summary: Install-time Windows DLL dropper with anti-forensic self-destruct
details: On `npm install`, the package's `install` lifecycle script (`lib/chart-loader.js`) fetches an encrypted blob from `https://github.com/Analysis-Chart/analysis-chart/releases/download/v1/payload.bin.enc`, XOR-decrypts it with key 0x42, base64-decodes the result, validates a Windows PE (MZ) header, writes the resulting DLL to `%APPDATA%\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\<random>.dll` (masquerading inside a Microsoft-named directory), and executes it via `spawn('rundll32.exe', [dllPath, 'Run'], { detached: true, stdio: 'ignore', windowsHide: true })`. After dropping the DLL, the script launches a detached `node -e` process that waits 3 seconds, recursively deletes the package's own directory, and rewrites the consumer's `package.json` to remove the `analysis-chart` dependency entry — erasing forensic traces while the persisted DLL keeps running. The package advertises itself as a 'lightweight chart analysis library' but ships zero analysis code; the install hook is the only functionality. This is a textbook auto-executed remote-payload dropper with anti-forensics on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
